name: Amazon
philosophy: Amazon's storefront is the textbook density-over-elegance e-commerce surface — Ember system font, dense product grids, yellow 'Add to Cart' as the brand voltage, conversion-engineered chrome where every pixel earns its place by lifting purchase rate. The atmosphere is utilitarian to the point of austerity; chrome is gray-on-white-on-blue, accents are amber-yellow and orange, and the product imagery does the heavy lifting.

design_language.motion_signature: Minimal motion — the storefront is engineered for fast load and zero distraction. Hover triggers slight underline on links and a 50ms color shift on buttons. No spring, no carousel auto-cycling beyond the homepage hero.

trademark_signals: Amazon orange-yellow CTAs (Add to Cart #ffd814, Buy Now #ffa41c) — the brand's conversion voltage. Reserved for purchase actions only
Amazon Ember system font — proprietary humanist sans, narrow proportions, runs display through body at small sizes
Dense product grids — 6+ products visible above the fold, with the famous '1-2-3 stack' of price, prime badge, rating
Navy-blue header bar (#131921) — the persistent chrome that holds search, account, cart
Star ratings as a visual language — 4.5/5 with the half-filled-star icon set in amber, review count in muted gray underneath
ai_slop_avoided: Don't replace the yellow Add-to-Cart with a brand-recolored CTA — the specific #ffd814 yellow is the conversion signal
Don't sparsify the product grid — Amazon's density is intentional, not a flaw to be 'fixed'
Don't add motion or hover transforms to product cards — the storefront optimizes for stillness
Don't replace Amazon Ember with Inter or a friendlier humanist sans — Ember's narrowness is part of the density
